meta.topic: Reporting offers, suggestions, orders, intentions, etc.

step_1_context.text: When we report offers, suggestions, orders, intentions, promises, requests, etc. we can follow some verbs in the reporting clause with:

step_2_explanation.simple_rule: When we report offers, suggestions, orders, intentions, promises, requests, etc.
step_2_explanation.formula:
After some verbs we can use a that-clause instead of a to-infinitive clause:
 • He promised to arrive on time.
step_2_explanation.common_mistake:
After the verbs insist, order, say and suggest we use a that-clause but not a to-infinitive clause:
 • There were cheers when he suggested that we went home early.

step_3_interactive_exercises.question: 36.1.1 'If I were you, I'd read the exam questions very carefully.' $\rightarrow$ He ___ to read the exam questions very carefully.
step_3_interactive_exercises.explanation_for_failure: Use 'advise' with an object to give a recommendation.

step_3_interactive_exercises.question: 36.1.2 'Okay, I'll collect Declan from school.' $\rightarrow$ He ___ to collect Declan from school.
step_3_interactive_exercises.explanation_for_failure: He expressed willingness or made a promise.

step_3_interactive_exercises.question: 36.2.1 The Health Minister suggested to improve the country's health care. (Replace 'suggested' if incorrect)
step_3_interactive_exercises.explanation_for_failure: 'Suggest' is not followed by a to-infinitive clause. It takes a that-clause or an -ing form.

step_3_interactive_exercises.question: 36.2.2 She intended that this could be done without raising taxes. (Replace 'intended' if incorrect)
36.3.1 To avoid the road works, police have advised ___ the motorway at Junction 3.
step_3_interactive_exercises.explanation_for_failure: Advise can be followed by an -ing clause when there is no object.
